title: WoG · Bonds 1.5
question: Wildbow, are all of your stories in the same multi-verse? Is there an alternate, unpowered Taylor or Casper somewhere in Pact world?
answer: Sure, why not? But they won’t show up.

title: WoG · Bonds 1.5
question:
This sequence may indicate an exploitable vulnerability:

“You were dealing with Maggie Holt,” I said. “Weren’t you?”

“That,” he said, raising a finger. He let his arm drop, “Wasn’t one of the things you saw. I’m positive.”

But Blake did
answer:
Laird told Blake Maggie’s last name.

Blake saw Maggie and Padraic in the vision, but not specifically making a deal. He indicated his suspicions that they -were- dealing with each other, however, which Padraic confirmed.

title: WoG · Bonds 1.1
question: Hey Wildbow – if it’s not too much trouble, can I have a WoG on the date for Granny’s death? I’m guessing it’s late August/early September of 2010, but if I have something concrete to start from I’ll be able to calculate the rest as you
answer:
August 11th, 2013.

The jump in the story moves you forward to December 5th, 2013.

title: WoG · Gathered Pages: 1
question:
A possible error. Two of the entries are labeled September 25, 1939.

I love the chapter and the story as a whole!
answer: Events are a continuation. She wrote in her diary, encountered him, they did battle, she wrote in her diary after.

title: WoG · Breach 3.5
question: For practicality purposes, whats the difference between a ring and a circle? I mean 3 dimensionally I would say sphere before a circle. For a ring, its boxed instead of circular? It has sides instead of X amount of distance away from the
answer: The ring doesn’t fill in all of the circle’s interior, leaving a circular space within untouched.

title: WoG · Collateral 4.1
question:
Bit confused on the count here.

“Joel, Goosh, Ty, Joseph, Amanda, Nick, Tiffany, and Stephen.

Then I counted heads.

Nine people, spread out through the apartment. Ten if I counted Alexis, smoking on the balcony.”

Where does Tiffany fit
answer: Just before Stephen. 😉

title: WoG · Histories (Arc 3)
question: Should we assume that the Japanese branch of the Duchamps make contracts／人◕ ‿‿ ◕人＼to become Puella Magi?
answer:
Not in my setting, no. For the record, Puella Magi exist across the entire world. Obviously.

/sanctimonious comic book guy tone

…I’m such a dork.

title: WoG · Collateral 4.7
question:
“Two more things to cover,” I said, “Unless you have ideas on what goes into a contract?”

“If I had specific knowledge,” Pauz said, “I would not be free.”

Last bit was probably meant to be “it would not be free.”
answer: Nope. If he knew the particulars of binding pacts, he would be bound, he means.

title: WoG · Collateral 4.8
question: Well they’re both armored creatures, to some extent, right? Beetles are hard to pin down – the amount of diversity between species is huge, which makes it tough to say anything definitive or useful. Crabs…as you said, more about scavenging
answer: Cold.

title: WoG · Collateral 4.9
question: That talk of covering up scars… self-harming? Not really a surprise, I had my suspicions.
answer: Nay.

title: WoG · Collateral 4.11
question: I’ve said it before, and I think now is a relevant time to say it again. Blake should’ve been a blue mage. If he was, this would be the perfect opportunity to learn the Goblin Punch command. It has a magic power cost of 0 and is super
answer: When is he ever fighting an opponent of his level?

title: WoG · Collateral 4.11
question: I thought I remembered somewhere that he couldn’t make a circle from the chain earlier. As a protective barrier. I guess I’m getting things muddled.
answer: He couldn’t be sure he’d be able to make a circle big enough to surround the Hyena.

title: WoG · Conviction 5.5
question:
Wow, potentially a big mistake, Rose.

Telling an agent of conquest the name of the demon your grandmother bound in your house. A demon that has apparently been known for centuries by name, and is a strong threat to other practitioners
answer: Keep in mind that Rose Sr. named Barbatorem, so the name doesn’t necessarily hold power. It’s like calling a dog spot.

title: WoG · Conviction 5.6
question: I don’t understand. How do they know that there were three allies?
answer: Jawbone (ctrl f), three incidents where the demon drew blood that wasn’t from Blake & Friends.

title: WoG · Conviction 5.6
question:
Ok. Theory time:

“Some practitioners have barometers, to measure where they stand in the grand scheme of things,” I said.

“Implements and the like, yes,” Fell said.

Blake actually already has an inplement, his tattoos. Either they
answer: I think you’re misunderstanding. “And the like” means “and similar objects.”

title: WoG · Subordination 6.4
question:
I do, in fact, like the internet. I use it for work, video, games and Pact. I would like the internet to improve, not go backwards.

A Maggie Holt Arc would be awesome. Would this take place alongside Pact, in the past, or an alternate
answer: Yes.

title: WoG · Subordination 6.7
question:
Very glad to see that Rose wasn’t thinking of something with an intrinsic “radiation,” but rather something else contained in their vast library.

Though now that I type that, I’m wondering why radiation wasn’t considered a tool for
answer: Not always easy to track. If Corvidae does something & causes a great deal of misery in the fallout, is that radiation or something else?

title: WoG · Signature 8.4
question:
Here is some food for thought, and I am quite serious about this:

What would happen if I said “The next sentence is true. The previous sentence is false”?

Do spirits understand recursion? Are spirits smart enough to recognise the
answer: Spirits punish you for being a pain in the ass.

title: WoG · Null 9.3
question:
Yeah. As soon as he realised Carl was sort of a part of him I got this track stuck in my head: https://www.youtube.com/watch?feature=player_detailpage&v=YK9Y1EqjDpY

Confront the shadow. . .

I am thou . . . Thou art I

Hang on a sec. Is
answer:
I’m not sure if I should be glad or bothered I haven’t played the Persona series. Might make it harder to write if I had to dodge around something that exists in another canon, but conversely, could help on a level, knowing what tropes to avoid/play off.

This isn’t really collective unconscious though.

title: WoG · Null 9.6
question: So, I don’t understand… He fought Ur in the physical world (or maybe the spirit world), but then he entered the Mirror world to get to Hillsglade House? I guess that means he can leave the mirror world as well, though, right? Or did he
answer: He didn’t fight Ur in the physical world.

title: WoG · Null 9.6
question:
Nice touch with the wording, how Blake had almost forgotten his face. You could have mentioned how for 9 arcs now Blake has been seeing Rose instead of himself, but that would have reminded us of the transition early.

Speaking of the
answer: He realized. See the line about him looking at the graffiti, and the ensuing change of tone.

title: WoG · Null 9.6
question:
Oh god that is he couldn’t take a shower, because the shower curtain would block the view of the mirror?

FML
answer: The mirror didn’t face the shower, but yeah.

title: WoG · Histories 9
question: OK, let’s play “Spot the Other!” The “rotted branches” Other is clearly Blake, the “fat and blood” one is clearly Barbatorem. Those are the two I caught, but does anyone recognize the rest?
answer: Not Barbatorem. Keep in mind that this is Johannes’ domain, so it’s not a lot of Others you’ve seen.

title: WoG · Mala Fide 10.3
question: They don’t. Google keeps thinking I mean Isaiah…
answer: It’s transcribed as it’s pronounced, not as it’s written out.

title: WoG · Malfeasance 11.2
question:
I tried to get away from it all, and I wound up in freefall. . . Yeah. I was windmilling my arms, looking for something to hold on to

Was Tiff lying? Doesn’t she have to add the addendum “figuratively speaking” or “so to say” or something
answer: At one point she was drunk, so she was technically right, freefalling and even windmilling her arms.

title: WoG · Malfeasance 11.4
question: Ok, I’m a little confused. is there now a copy of The Hyena in the real world? Blake duplicated it in his Realm, right? If he gets the original back, could he dual wield Goblin Swords?
answer: He got the original back. Sandra tried to pick it up, he used the Sympathy magic to get it close enough to a surface that he could reach through (breaking the surface) and collect it.

title: WoG · Malfeasance 11.4
question:
That makes sense. I see it now.

Did the Hyena’s reflection disappear when Blake reclaimed the original, or does he now have 2?
answer: Yep.

title: WoG · Malfeasance 11.4
question:
The contest itself was awesome, very nice choreography.

But I don’t really understand what were the rules.

So it sounds like they’re agreeing not to permanently harm each-other, and not to be interrupted? But then:

But if he couldn’t
answer:
No interference = no interference should happen with the unpowered locals. I guess that’s unclear.

If Blake hurts Alister and Alister couldn’t heal it, it would be because Alister lied. The onus would be very much on Alister, as would the consequences.

title: WoG · Malfeasance 11.7
question: Wildbow, just thought I would let you know that tasers emphatically do not work like they are depicted here. Like, not at all. First of all, if you’re not using the barbs that pierce the skin, as in, if you’re “shoving” with your taser,
answer: He doesn’t knock them out with the taser. He zaps them and then stays near them – he remains within two steps of Ellie from the time he zaps her and lets her collapse against the wall. She doesn’t scream because he covers her mouth with one hand.

title: WoG · Duress 12.1
question:
Three parts I was confused about:

1.) “I glanced at Peter. He’d dropped one line, which made me want to ask a hundred questions I didn’t have the time to ask. I wasn’t sure I didn’t share the same sentiment.”

Share what same sentiment?
answer:
1) See the statement preceding that paragraph.

2) You did miss something.

3) Yes.

title: WoG · Duress 12.5
question:
Drywall and dust ingrained into the skin, maybe a few nasty reactions with blood from the mouth, nasal inflammation or rubbing the eyes too much to create the makeup. Lack of speech like bat!Blake.

Carnivorous due to the lack of anything
answer: Or they just ate something funny.

title: WoG · Duress 12.7
question: Those 3 scouts, are they Krasue/Penanggalan/Leyak from Southeast Asia or Rokurokubi from Japan?
answer: Yes.

title: WoG · Duress 12.7
question: Wait, is she a Toon? There are Toons in the Pactverse? I wonder if Hatsune Miku is an Other as well, then.
answer: Not a Toon. Just as an image like a reflection in the mirror can be conveyed to reality, an image like a segment of film can do the same.

title: WoG · Duress 12.8
question:
Maybe i am bad at reading, but still:

2 women, 3 men.

2 women (Joyce and Gail) went away, then there are 4 people left, one of them is a woman (Jan)

Uh what?
answer:
They were a group of 5: Beard1, Beard2, Necromancer, Gail and Jan.

Joyce was standing a distance away.

title: WoG · Void 7.2
question:
‘dusting his hair and pubes white’

Pubes are pubic hair. As in, on the pelvic bone. Do you mean ‘beard hairs’ or ‘chin hair’ or anything that doesn’t make me think of genitals?
answer: He’s naked. I’m talking genitals there.

title: WoG · Execution 13.4
question: How long is Craig going to go on about killing Laird like its new? (Note that I’m not complaining that he’s justified in his anger or the occasional reminder since I bring up the same points, it’s just that at this point he’s told everyone
answer: Tenements.

title: WoG · Execution 13.4
question: So on an unrelated note, I’m just kinda wondering. Is there a Brockton Bay in the Pactverse?
answer: Perhaps there was, once. But when things get bad enough, the practitioners just set up wards and leave a place to fall off the grid.

title: WoG · Malfeasance 11.4
question: Bizzare plot hole – If he hadn’t declared war Alistar would have been a liar and lost right there. I have noticed that the author tends to really shove the idiot ball down Blakes throat to keep the story going.
answer: If he hadn’t declared war and had backed down, Molly would have gotten upset, and the bell would have gotten agitated. More on this later.

title: WoG · Sine Die 14.8
question: Wait, what? Johannes was the obvious answer, but Alister was quite certain he wasn’t the correct one, courtesy of his cards. Could be a multi-part thing; Johannes isn’t doing it and doesn’t control an Other who is doing it, but he has been
answer: She knew the answers, she was just lying.

title: WoG · Possession 15.2
question: I’m really starting to dislike Blake and his suicidal desires and lack of any feeling of self-worth. Rose is a cruel, self-absorbed, arrogant, selfish bitch, and she has wronged and fucked over Blake on so many levels so many times.
answer: It’s less about saving her and more about ‘it’s better that one of us live than both of us go down fighting each other’.

title: WoG · Possession 15.5
question:
Oh for fucks sake Peter, don’t become a creeper to a teenager. Whatever he’s doing Alister should just beat the fuck out of him for it on principle.

Wait, what? Feed what? WHAT?!
answer: He’s only a year older than Rose, and Ainsley isn’t that far off from Alister’s age.

title: WoG · Judgment 16.12
question:
Rose was pretty awesome in this chapter. She’s earned all the clout she throws around.

Peter’s cool. I hope he survives.?

Christopher? Whatever happened to those in The Firm losing their identities?
answer: Lawyers lose their identities. Demons don’t.

title: WoG · Judgment 16.13
question: Was it ever explained why Rose and Blake are same-handed? xP Doesn’t make sense if they were cut from the Barber.
answer: Yes it was.

title: WoG · Epilogue
question: Hmmm… implying that Evan is hanging out with Joanna as a sparrow? Sucks that it seems the Duchamps were never able to retrieve her for whatever reason. But I presume that they turned Padraic into a piñata in the process of freeing her.
answer: It wasn’t Joanna. But good assumption on the latter half.

title: Pact — About
answer:
Interested in reading without even the slightest spoiler? Click here.

Blake Thorburn was driven away from home and family by a vicious fight over inheritance, returning only for a deathbed visit with the grandmother who set it in motion. Blake soon finds himself next in line to inherit the property, a trove of dark supernatural knowledge, and the many
